name: inanc-tekstil
description: Odanda Gör — AI room visualizer. Customers upload a room photo, select a curtain, and fal-ai/nano-banana-pro generates a new version of the room with the curtain placed. Quality-evaluated by Claude Opus 4.6 via Amazon Bedrock (BAML), auto-retries up to 3x. Entry: fullscreen modal on product pages + dedicated /pages/odanda-gor Shopify landing page for Reels ads.

userStories.title: Backend project scaffold — Bun/Hono server with CORS, body limit, logger
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the room-visualizer backend scaffolded as a Bun/Hono project so subsequent stories have a working foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory technical/room-visualizer/ created with package.json, tsconfig.json, Dockerfile
Hono server in src/index.ts with CORS (allow origin https://inanctekstil.store), bodyLimit(12MB), OPTIONS preflight handler

src/config.ts reads env vars: FAL_API_KEY, FAL_MODEL, SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN, SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_REGION; SCORE_THRESHOLD=7, MAX_ATTEMPTS=3, FAL_TIMEOUT_MS=45000
src/lib/logger.ts exports a pino structured JSON logger
GET /health endpoint returns 200 {ok: true}
bun run typecheck passes
bun run lint passes

userStories.title: DNS record + Docker compose entry for room-visualizer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the infrastructure configured so the room-visualizer service is reachable at visualizer.inanctekstil.store.
acceptanceCriteria: New hcloud_zone_record resource 'visualizer' added to technical/gitopsprod/dns.tf: name=visualizer, type=A, value=5.75.165.158

New service 'room-visualizer' added to /opt/inanctekstil/docker-compose.yml (or local docker-compose reference file at technical/room-visualizer/docker-compose.service.yml) with Traefik labels for visualizer.inanctekstil.store, TLS, and buffering.maxRequestBodyBytes=15728640
Environment variables documented in technical/room-visualizer/README.md: FAL_API_KEY, FAL_MODEL, SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN, SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_REGION
README.md includes deploy commands: rsync + docker compose up -d --build room-visualizer + terraform apply
bun run typecheck passes
bun run lint passes

userStories.title: Shopify + PMS product data fetcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a module that fetches curtain product data (title, image, metafields) from Shopify and optionally PMS so the prompt builder has the data it needs.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/shopify.ts fetches product by GID using Shopify Admin GraphQL API: returns { title, type (curtain.type metafield), color (curtain.color metafield), imageUrl (first product image) }
src/lib/pms.ts attempts to fetch swatch image URL for the product SKU from PMS at http://pms.inanctekstil.store; returns null on failure (non-blocking)
If PMS returns a swatch URL it is preferred over Shopify image; otherwise Shopify image used
getProductData(productId: string) exported from src/lib/shopify.ts returns combined data
Returns null (not throws) if product not found in Shopify
tests/shopify.test.ts: mocks fetch, tests product found, product not found, PMS fallback
bun run typecheck passes
bun run lint passes

userStories.title: Prompt builder — curtain data to fal.ai prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt builder module that constructs the fal.ai image+text prompt from curtain product data and adjusts it on retries.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/prompt-builder.ts exports buildPrompt(product: ProductData, attempt: number): { prompt: string, seed: number }
Attempt 1 base prompt: 'A modern Turkish living room with {color} {type} curtains hanging on the windows, photorealistic, interior design photography, natural lighting, high quality'
Attempt 2+: appends ', curtains prominently visible on windows, window treatment focal point'
seed is random on each call (Math.random based, deterministic in tests via injection)
tests/prompt-builder.test.ts: base prompt structure, retry prompt includes prominence text, different seeds per call
bun run typecheck passes
bun run lint passes

userStories.title: BAML evaluator — Claude Opus 4.6 quality scoring via Bedrock
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a BAML-defined evaluation function that uses Claude Opus 4.6 (Amazon Bedrock) to score generated room images.
acceptanceCriteria: baml_src/clients.baml defines ClaudeOpus client using aws-bedrock provider with model 'anthropic.claude-opus-4-6' and region from env.AWS_REGION
baml_src/evaluator.baml defines EvaluateRoomImage(image: image, product_name: string) -> EvaluationResult with fields: score (int 1-10), has_curtains (bool), feedback (string)
BAML client generated (baml_client/ directory)
src/lib/evaluator.ts exports evaluateImage(imageBuffer: Buffer, productName: string): Promise<EvaluationResult> using generated BAML client
tests/evaluator.test.ts: mocks BAML client, verifies evaluateImage returns typed result
bun run typecheck passes
bun run lint passes

userStories.title: fal.ai client with retry loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a fal.ai client that calls nano-banana-pro, downloads the result binary, evaluates quality, and retries up to 3 times.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/fal.ts exports generateRoomImage(roomImageBuffer: Buffer, productData: ProductData): Promise<{ binary: Buffer, score: number, attemptsUsed: number }>
Calls fal-ai/nano-banana-pro (from FAL_MODEL config) with room photo + curtain image URL + text prompt
Per-attempt timeout: FAL_TIMEOUT_MS (45000ms); timeout counts as failed attempt
Immediately downloads fal.ai CDN image as binary Buffer after each generation
Calls evaluateImage() on downloaded binary; retries if score < SCORE_THRESHOLD (7) OR has_curtains == false AND attempts < MAX_ATTEMPTS (3)
Stores all attempt binaries in memory (scoped to function call); returns Buffer with highest score
Logs each fal_call, fal_downloaded, eval_result event via logger
tests/fal.test.ts: mocks fal.ai and evaluator; tests 3-attempt max; has_curtains=false triggers retry; returns highest-scored binary not last
bun run typecheck passes
bun run lint passes

userStories.title: POST /api/visualize route — full pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the main visualize endpoint that accepts a room photo + product ID, runs the full AI pipeline, and returns the generated image binary.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routes/visualize.ts handles POST /api/visualize (multipart/form-data)
Validates: product_id present, room_image file present, file size <= 10MB, file type jpg/png/webp

Returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR for invalid input, 404 PRODUCT_NOT_FOUND if getProductData returns null, 429 RATE_LIMITED (5 req/min per IP, in-memory), 502 GENERATION_FAILED if fal returns error after all retries
On success: returns image/jpeg binary with headers X-Product-Title (percent-encoded), X-Attempts-Used, X-Final-Score
Logs visualize_start and visualize_complete events with duration_ms
CORS headers present on all responses including error responses
tests/visualize.test.ts: CORS headers on 400/404/502; multipart parsing; VALIDATION_ERROR cases; PRODUCT_NOT_FOUND; GENERATION_FAILED; success returns binary
bun run typecheck passes
bun run lint passes

userStories.title: Frontend build setup — esbuild + TypeScript config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Shopify theme configured with an esbuild build pipeline for TypeScript so room-visualizer.ts compiles to a browser-compatible IIFE.
acceptanceCriteria: technical/theme/package.json created with build script: esbuild assets/room-visualizer.ts --bundle --format=iife --target=es2017 --outfile=assets/room-visualizer.js
technical/theme/tsconfig.json created with target ES2017, strict: true, noEmit: true
Placeholder technical/theme/assets/room-visualizer.ts created (minimal — just exports an empty init function)
npm run build in technical/theme/ completes without errors and produces room-visualizer.js
room-visualizer.js is valid IIFE format (starts with (function(){) or similar)
Both room-visualizer.ts and room-visualizer.js committed to repo

userStories.title: Room visualizer modal — all 5 states in TypeScript
userStories.description: As a customer, I want the room visualizer modal to guide me from photo upload through curtain selection to viewing and saving the AI-generated result.
acceptanceCriteria: technical/theme/assets/room-visualizer.ts implements initRoomVisualizer(config: { productId?: string, productHandle?: string, apiUrl: string, dataProductsJson?: string }) exported function
Calling initRoomVisualizer() injects the modal into document.body on first call; subsequent calls re-open it
State 1 Upload: file input (jpg/png/webp, max 10MB) + camera capture on mobile (accept=image/* capture=environment); client-side size/format validation shows inline error before proceeding

State 2 Curtain Picker: shown only when config.productId is absent; reads products from config.dataProductsJson (JSON array of {id, handle, title, imageUrl, type}); grouped by type; tap selects and stores {id, handle} in modal state
State 3 Loading: shows uploaded photo dimmed + 'Yapay zeka odanızı hazırlıyor' text

State 4 Result: renders generated image from Blob URL (URL.createObjectURL); shows percent-decoded X-Product-Title; Kaydet button downloads via anchor; Sipariş Ver closes modal + navigates to /{handle} (dedicated page) or scrolls to configurator (product page)
State 5 Error: shows 'Görsel oluşturulamadı. Lütfen tekrar deneyin.' + Tekrar Dene button returning to State 1
API call: POST multipart/form-data to config.apiUrl with product_id + room_image file
npm run build in technical/theme/ passes
shopify theme push --theme 193714913361 --store 1z7hb1-2d.myshopify.com --only 'assets/room-visualizer.js' --nodelete --path technical/theme succeeds
Verified in browser via Playwright MCP: open /pages/odanda-gor preview, click Hemen Dene, modal opens in State 1

userStories.title: Product page — Odanda Gör button snippet
userStories.description: As a customer browsing a product page, I want an 'Odanda Gör' button that opens the visualizer modal with that product pre-selected.
acceptanceCriteria: technical/theme/snippets/room-visualizer-button.liquid created; renders a button 'Odanda Gör' that calls initRoomVisualizer with the current product.id and product.handle
Button only rendered when product.tags contains 'odanda-gor'

Script tag in snippet loads room-visualizer.js and calls initRoomVisualizer({ productId: '{{product.admin_graphql_api_id}}', productHandle: '{{product.handle}}', apiUrl: 'https://visualizer.inanctekstil.store' })
Snippet included in technical/theme/templates/product.json (placed after product images, before configurator)
shopify theme push --theme 193714913361 --store 1z7hb1-2d.myshopify.com --only 'snippets/room-visualizer-button.liquid' --only 'templates/product.json' --nodelete --path technical/theme succeeds
Verified in browser via Playwright MCP: navigate to a product tagged 'odanda-gor', Odanda Gör button visible, clicking opens modal in State 1 (picker skipped)

userStories.title: Dedicated /pages/odanda-gor Shopify landing page
userStories.description: As a customer arriving from an Instagram Reels ad, I want a clear landing page that explains the feature and lets me launch the visualizer with a curtain picker.
acceptanceCriteria: A Shopify page with handle 'odanda-gor' exists (create via Admin or note it must be created manually)

technical/theme/sections/odanda-gor-page.liquid created as a custom section with: hero headline 'Perdenizi Odanızda Görün', 3-step explainer (Fotoğraf yükle / Perde seç / Yapay zeka eklesin), 'Hemen Dene' button

Section uses Liquid to inline product data as JSON in a data-products attribute: {% assign curtain_products = collections['all'].products | where: 'type', 'Perde' %} (or equivalent to get TUL/FON/BLACKOUT products); serialized as [{id, handle, title, imageUrl, type}]
Hemen Dene button calls initRoomVisualizer({ apiUrl: 'https://visualizer.inanctekstil.store', dataProductsJson: '...' }) — no productId so picker is shown
Section schema defined so it can be added to a page template
shopify theme push --theme 193714913361 --store 1z7hb1-2d.myshopify.com --only 'sections/odanda-gor-page.liquid' --nodelete --path technical/theme succeeds
Verified in browser via Playwright MCP: navigate to /pages/odanda-gor preview URL, page renders hero + 3-step explainer + Hemen Dene button, clicking opens modal in State 2 (curtain picker visible)
